Product ladder

A practical product now. A public beta for persistent intelligence taking shape in the open.

Live now

Spaces

Structured AI workspaces you can use right now. Projects stay separated, context stays clean, and daily work does not dissolve into generic chat threads. This is the layer the current app may still call ZAKI Chat.

$13/mo

Start now

Experimental — free

ZAKI

Most AI resets with every session. ZAKI explores what happens when it does not: continuity, memory, and a long-running relationship. When the work needs writing, research, or organized execution, you move into Spaces.

Free

Try it now
Experimental

ZAKI is experimental by design. It is our public test of persistent personal intelligence: memory continuity, visible work phases, and per-user context that does not reset every time you start over.

Public beta is open now with 5 messages every 24 hours
Free during beta, limited by design, and intentionally early
This is where we test the next product shape in public

Why most AI still feels generic

Most AI stops at answers. ZAKI starts with disciplined work in Spaces, then moves toward persistent intelligence that remembers context instead of resetting every session.

Nova Nuggets Team

The product today

What is ZAKI, exactly?

ZAKI is the persistent agent. Spaces are the live paid workspaces for organized daily productivity. ZAKI is also the experimental public beta for persistent personal intelligence — the long-thread relationship behind the work.

Product map

  • Spaces→ live, paid workspaces for focused execution
  • ZAKI→ experimental beta for a persistent personal agent
  • Nullalis→ private runtime layer behind continuity and memory

Why persistence matters

Context is what separates a tool from a counterpart

The real difference is not one good answer. It is whether AI stays useful over time. ZAKI starts with structured chat, then builds a continuity and memory layer on top.

  • Long-running work needs context that does not disappear between sessions.
  • Memory changes whether AI helps once or improves with repeated use.

Source

Anthropic reports that context management and memory improve agent performance on complex, multi-step tasks.

Anthropic: Managing context

What makes ZAKI different

Persistent intelligence, not generic output

Spaces are optimized for structured work, not endless generic output. ZAKI adds what ordinary chat does not: memory continuity, per-user context, and visible work phases.

  • ZAKI is built around continuity first, not as a bolt-on later.

Built-in

Memory continuity

Default

Per-user isolation

What users feel is not the runtime label. It is the result: continuity, memory, and a clearer working relationship with AI.

Pricing

Use Spaces today. Explore ZAKI now.

Spaces are the paid workspaces you can use right now. ZAKI is the public beta for persistent personal intelligence — free during beta with 5 messages every 24 hours. Subscriptions start after we learn from this phase.

Live

Spaces

Monthly

$13

For everyday work, bilingual writing, and execution inside organized workspaces for each project. This is the product the app may still call ZAKI Chat, but on the website we explain it more clearly as Spaces.

Chat

Paid

Model

Premium models with richer reasoning
Priority responses and stronger context
More personal memory and assistant-style support

ZAKI

Experimental

Free

During beta

ZAKI is the public beta for persistent personal intelligence. It keeps context between sessions, shows its work phases, and gives you 5 messages every 24 hours to test the direction before it becomes a full subscription product.

5

Msgs / day

Free

During beta

Experimental

State

5 messages every 24 hours — resets daily
Persistent memory that carries across sessions
Visible work phases — you see what ZAKI is doing
Free now. Paid subscriptions start after beta.

Early access

Your email is only used for ZAKI beta updates and early-access communication. No marketing spam.

Join the Beta

From early users

What early users told us to improve

These are representative requests and feedback themes we kept hearing from early users and testers while shaping the product.

47votes

Let ZAKI remember my writing style across sessions — so drafts feel like mine, not generic AI output.

Memory
Nora M.·2 days ago
38votes

Arabic-first summarization that actually understands context, not just word-by-word translation.

Arabic
Anonymous·3 days ago
31votes

A daily briefing mode — ZAKI checks my calendar, emails, and gives me a 30-second morning summary.

Operator
Khalid R.·5 days ago
26votes

Integration with Notion and Google Docs so ZAKI can pull context from where I actually work.

Integration
Sara A.·1 week ago
19votes

Voice input with real-time transcription — especially for Arabic dialects.

Voice
Anonymous·1 week ago

Early requests that helped shape what comes next
